rule: Tamil cinema climax scenes need escalating tension in dialogue. Start soft, build pressure, then one final devastating line.
Introduction scenes (hero entry) should have exactly one mass dialogue. Less is more. The BGM carries the rest.
Confrontation scenes alternate between short and long lines. The villain speaks more, the hero speaks less. Silence from the hero = power.
Separation scenes (lovers/family parting) should have one character speaking while the other is silent. The silent character's reaction is the scene.
Flashback emotional scenes use past tense and present tense alternating. 'Andha naal...' followed by 'Ippo...' creates temporal contrast.
Revelation scenes (truth comes out) are where subtext becomes text. The character finally says what they've been hiding. Maximum impact.
Rain scenes in Tamil cinema amplify emotion. Dialogue during rain should be louder (mass) or whispered (emotion). Rain = external mirror of internal state.
Action scene dialogue must be under 10 words. In between punches. No time for speeches.
Slow emotional scenes should have long pauses between lines. Each line is a paragraph of feeling compressed into one sentence.
Dinner table scenes are perfect for subtext. Everyone talks about food while the real conversation happens underneath.
